title: Bidifferential specialization
summary: Specializes multi-input notation to two slots, with weights (w₁,w₂), paired inputs (f,g), and expected output weight w₁+w₂−2N.

mathematicalContent.heading: Two inputs and two weights
mathematicalContent.body: A bidifferential input is a pair (f,g). The weight function on Fin 2 stores w₁ in slot zero and w₂ in slot one.

mathematicalContent.heading: Readable predicates
mathematicalContent.body: The generic multi-homogeneity and tangentiality predicates are renamed for the two-input case without changing their content.

mathematicalContent.heading: Expected output weight
mathematicalContent.body: For total Laplacian degree N, the bookkeeping output weight is the sum of the two input weights minus 2N.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Abbreviates a rank-two multi-input, mathematically an ordered pair of ambient functions.
Builds the two-slot weight vector with w1 in slot zero and w2 in slot one.
correspondence.leanType: def biWeights (_CalConf : Calculus Conf) (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : Fin 2 → ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Builds the rank-two input whose left entry is f and right entry is g.
correspondence.leanType: def pairInput (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.BiInput

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Projects the zero, or left, component of a bidifferential input.
Projects the one, or right, component of a bidifferential input.
Renames generic rank-two tangentiality at the weight pair (w1,w2).
correspondence.leanType: def IsBiTangentialAtWeights (B : CalConf.BiOperator) (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Renames generic multi-homogeneity for a two-input tuple.
correspondence.leanType: def IsBiHomogeneous (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) (F : CalConf.BiInput) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the expected bidifferential output weight using the generic outputWeight function.
correspondence.leanType: def biOutputWeight (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) (N : ℕ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The zero slot of the two-weight vector is w1.
correspondence.leanType: theorem biWeights_zero (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : CalConf.biWeights w₁ w₂ 0 = w₁

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The one slot of the two-weight vector is w2.
correspondence.leanType: theorem biWeights_one (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : CalConf.biWeights w₁ w₂ 1 = w₂

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The zero slot of pairInput(f,g) is f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem pairInput_zero (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.pairInput f g 0 = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The one slot of pairInput(f,g) is g.
correspondence.leanType: theorem pairInput_one (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.pairInput f g 1 = g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Left projection recovers f from the pair (f,g).
correspondence.leanType: theorem leftInput_pairInput (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.leftInput (CalConf.pairInput f g) = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Right projection recovers g from the pair (f,g).
correspondence.leanType: theorem rightInput_pairInput (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.rightInput (CalConf.pairInput f g) = g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The total weight of the two-slot vector is the sum of its entries.
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalWeight_biWeights (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : CalConf.totalWeight (CalConf.biWeights w₁ w₂) = w₁ + w₂

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expands the expected bidifferential output weight.
correspondence.leanType: theorem biOutputWeight_eq (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) (N : ℕ) : CalConf.biOutputWeight w₁ w₂ N = w₁ + w₂ - 2 * (N : ℝ)

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: This module is a notation layer. It does not define a particular bidifferential formula, prove that a BiOperator is bilinear or differential, or establish its homogeneity and tangentiality. biOutputWeight is only the expected bookkeeping value.
